audit: pzoom static-analysis findings on the Psalm codebase

analyzer.branch: claude/dreamy-wright-na9bh5 (merged main thru #38; plus two offender fixes below)

fixes_applied_in_this_change: PossiblyUnusedReturnValue on interface/abstract methods (offender #2, 9 FPs): using an override's return value now also marks the overridden parent/interface method's return used (atomic_method_call_analyzer.rs), mirroring the existing referenced-member propagation.

PossiblyUnusedProperty/UnusedProperty on write-only STATIC properties (part of offender #1, 8 FPs): a static-property write now counts as a reference (static_property_assignment_analyzer.rs), matching Psalm (which counts any static-property access; instance writes still don't count).
methodology: Ran the release pzoom binary on the Psalm project (vendor installed via composer; Psalm's own psalm-baseline.xml applied, as pzoom does automatically). Ground truth for 'what Psalm itself reports' was obtained by running Psalm on its own codebase with --ignore-baseline (1659 findings, all otherwise baselined). Each pzoom issue was compared against Psalm's findings and verified by reading the PHP source; several semantic questions (write-only static properties, count()/non-empty narrowing, and the always-truthy RedundantCondition idiom) were settled with controlled single-file experiments run through both tools.

classification_legend.false_positive: pzoom is wrong: no real problem, or a pzoom analysis/stub/parsing/suppression-scope bug. Psalm correctly stays silent.
classification_legend.false_negative: the visible issue is a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress, but it is the symptom of a pzoom false NEGATIVE: pzoom fails to emit the underlying suppressed check (which Psalm does emit) and therefore thinks the @psalm-suppress is unused. The fix is to implement the missing check.
classification_legend.true_issue_psalm_misses: pzoom correctly found a genuine issue in the PHP code that Psalm does NOT report (pzoom is more precise here).
classification_legend.real_but_psalm_suppresses_or_baselines: pzoom's finding is correct AND Psalm's own analysis produces it too, but Psalm intentionally suppresses it inline or via psalm-baseline.xml; pzoom surfaces it only because it does not honor that suppression/baseline entry (a suppression/baseline-matching gap, not an analysis error).

count_note: 101 issues. History: 119 at first audit; #36 (value-of<self::CONST> docblock fix) removed the UnusedDocblockParam FP -> 118; merging main thru #38 swapped 2 TypeCombiner UnusedPsalmSuppress false-negatives (now emitted) for 2 TKeyedArray PossiblyInvalidPropertyAssignmentValue list-shape FPs (net 118); then the two fixes in this change removed 17 FPs (9 return-value + 8 static-property) -> 101. Psalm's composer.lock is git-ignored so vendor versions float; a few findings (e.g. the PHPUnit InvocationMocker group) depend on exact vendor code.
false_negative_detail: All 59 are UnusedPsalmSuppress. Each marks a `@psalm-suppress X` that Psalm needs (Psalm emits X at that spot, then the suppression consumes it). pzoom does not yet emit X, so it reports the suppression as unused. By suppressed-issue type X, the checks pzoom is missing are dominated by: ArgumentTypeCoercion, MixedAssignment/MixedOperand/MixedArgumentTypeCoercion, RiskyTruthyFalsyComparison, ImpureFunctionCall/ImpureStaticProperty, PropertyTypeCoercion, PossiblyNull*/PossiblyFalse*/PossiblyInvalid* and several Redundant/More/LessSpecific* checks.
true_issue_psalm_misses_detail: All 11 are RedundantCondition / RedundantConditionGivenDocblockType on the idiom `<cond> && ($x = $obj->getArray()|getAtomicTypes()|getSingleAtomic())`. Those getters are @psalm-mutation-free and return non-falsable types (objects / non-empty-array), so the truthiness test is always true — a genuine redundant condition. pzoom catches these; Psalm does not (confirmed by the project author and by a controlled reproduction in which Psalm is silent while pzoom emits the identical message). These are pzoom being MORE precise than Psalm.

issues.message: Suppression of ArgumentTypeCoercion is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress ArgumentTypeCoercion` is genuinely required — Psalm emits ArgumentTypeCoercion here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit ArgumentTypeCoercion at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit ArgumentTypeCoercion here but does not; closing that gap would both surface ArgumentTypeCoercion and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to property Psalm\CodeLocation::$docblock_start
issues.explanation: CodeLocation::$docblock_start IS read at CodeLocation.php:147 `$this->preview_start = $this->docblock_start ?: $this->file_start;`. pzoom misses this constructor-context read.

issues.message: The return value for this method is never used
issues.explanation: verifyReturnType() returns false|null and carries `@psalm-suppress PossiblyUnusedReturnValue unused but seems important` (line 74). Psalm emits this issue and the inline suppress consumes it. pzoom anchors the issue on the @return docblock line (73) which falls OUTSIDE the suppression scope it computes for the function, so it fails to honor the co-located suppress. Finding is real; pzoom should suppress it.

issues.message: This method’s complexity is greater than the project limit (method graph size = 874, average path length = 118)

issues.message: Type Psalm\Type\Atomic for $arg_value_type->getsingleatomic() is always truthy
issues.explanation: Valid finding. `getSingleAtomic()` is @psalm-mutation-free and returns a non-falsable Atomic, so the && operand `($atomic_arg_type = $arg_value_type->getSingleAtomic())` is always truthy — a genuine redundant truthiness test (the assignment still binds the var, but the test never fails). Confirmed by the project author. Psalm does NOT flag the always-truthy-ness of a mutation-free method result captured in an assignment-within-a-condition: verified by a controlled reproduction (Psalm silent, pzoom emits the identical 'Type Atom for $this->getSingle() is always truthy') and by Psalm emitting zero such findings codebase-wide (none baselined, no inline suppress). pzoom correctly catches what Psalm misses.

issues.message: Possibly undefined array offset '1' is risky given expected type 'int'. Consider using isset beforehand.
issues.explanation: Psalm emits this same PossiblyUndefinedIntArrayOffset and it is covered by psalm-baseline.xml. pzoom emits it because its extracted snippet text does not match the baseline <code> entry, so the baseline filter misses it. The underlying finding matches Psalm.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to property Psalm\Internal\Analyzer\Statements\Expression\Call\FunctionCallInfo::$byref_uses
issues.explanation: FunctionCallInfo::$byref_uses IS read via compound assignment at FunctionCallAnalyzer.php:707 `$function_call_info->byref_uses += $var_type_part->byref_uses;` (the `+=` reads the current value). pzoom does not count the read side of a compound assignment.

issues.message: Possibly undefined array offset '1' is risky given expected type 'int'. Consider using isset beforehand.
issues.explanation: Guarded by `if ($function_id === 'preg_replace' && count($stmt->getArgs()) > 2)`. After count()>2, offsets 0/1/2 exist, so getArgs()[1] is defined. pzoom does not narrow list length via the count()>N guard.

issues.message: Suppression of ComplexMethod is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress ComplexMethod` is genuinely required — Psalm emits ComplexMethod here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit ComplexMethod at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit ComplexMethod here but does not; closing that gap would both surface ComplexMethod and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: Possibly undefined array offset '0' is risky given expected type 'int'. Consider using isset beforehand.
issues.explanation: Guarded by `if ($stmt->getArgs())` (line 356) which makes getArgs() a non-empty list, so getArgs()[0] is defined. pzoom does not narrow to non-empty through the truthiness guard on the (pure, memoized) getArgs() call.

issues.message: This method’s complexity is greater than the project limit (method graph size = 831, average path length = 145)
issues.explanation: pzoom's complexity calculation crosses the limit for a method Psalm keeps under the limit. Psalm flags different methods (Config.php:927, ArgumentAnalyzer.php:712). Metric divergence, not a code defect.

issues.message: Argument 1 of Psalm\Type\Atomic\TKeyedArray::__construct expects non-empty-array<int|string, Psalm\Type\Union>, but parent type array<int|string, Psalm\Type\Union> provided
issues.explanation: `new TKeyedArray($left->properties + $right->properties, null)` — TKeyedArray::$properties is non-empty-array<string|int,Union>. `non-empty-array + non-empty-array` stays non-empty (left keys are preserved), which matches the constructor's non-empty-array param. pzoom's `+` operator loses the non-empty-array-ness, yielding plain array.

issues.message: Suppression of ArgumentTypeCoercion is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress ArgumentTypeCoercion` is genuinely required — Psalm emits ArgumentTypeCoercion here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit ArgumentTypeCoercion at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit ArgumentTypeCoercion here but does not; closing that gap would both surface ArgumentTypeCoercion and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: The declared return type 'string' for Psalm\Internal\ExecutionEnvironment\GitInfoCollector::collectBranch is not nullable, but 'null|string' contains null
issues.explanation: collectBranch(): string returns `$exploded[1]` from `explode('* ', $result, 2)` where $result is guarded by `str_starts_with($result, '* ')`, so index 1 always exists and is a string. pzoom adds `null` for a possibly-undefined int offset (and ignores the str_starts_with guard / explode's guaranteed 2 parts), so it sees null|string.

issues.message: The type null|string does not match the declared return type string
issues.explanation: Same root cause as the InvalidNullableReturnType above: `return $exploded[1];` is provably a string (the '* ' separator is present), but pzoom models the possibly-undefined offset as nullable.

issues.message: Cannot resolve the given expression to a file path
issues.explanation: `$callable = require $argv[0];` is intentional dynamic loading of a worker script path. Psalm (which has the UnresolvableInclude check) reports MixedAssignment here but deliberately does NOT emit UnresolvableInclude for a plain-variable include. pzoom over-eagerly flags the dynamic require.

issues.message: Suppression of MixedAssignment is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress MixedAssignment` is genuinely required — Psalm emits MixedAssignment here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit MixedAssignment at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit MixedAssignment here but does not; closing that gap would both surface MixedAssignment and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to private property Psalm\Internal\LanguageServer\ProtocolStreamReader::$buffer
issues.explanation: ProtocolStreamReader::$buffer IS read at lines 80/87/88/96 (`str_ends_with($this->buffer,...)`, `explode(':', $this->buffer)`, `strlen($this->buffer)`). pzoom misses these reads (the property is mutated with `.=` then read).

issues.message: Docblock-defined type Psalm\Type\Atomic\TArray|Psalm\Type\Atomic\TClassStringMap|Psalm\Type\Atomic\TKeyedArray for $first_arg_type->getarray() is always truthy
issues.explanation: Valid finding Psalm misses. The && operand `... && ($x = $obj->getArray()/getAtomicTypes())` captures the result of a @psalm-mutation-free getter whose docblock return type is non-falsable (getArray() -> TArray|TKeyedArray|TClassStringMap, all objects; getAtomicTypes() -> non-empty-array), so the truthiness test is always true — a genuine redundant condition. Confirmed by the project author. Psalm reports zero RedundantConditionGivenDocblockType codebase-wide (none baselined, no inline suppress) and is silent on a controlled reproduction of this exact pattern, whereas pzoom emits it. pzoom's RedundantCondition analysis is more precise than Psalm's here.

issues.message: Possibly undefined array offset '0' is risky given expected type 'int|string'. Consider using isset beforehand.
issues.explanation: Psalm emits this same offset issue; it is in psalm-baseline.xml. pzoom's snippet text differs from the baseline <code>, so pzoom does not suppress it. Finding matches Psalm.

issues.message: Argument 1 of Psalm\Type\Atomic\TArray::__construct expects list{Psalm\Type\Union, Psalm\Type\Union}, but list{Psalm\Type\Union, Psalm\Type\Union, ...<Psalm\Type\Union>} provided
issues.explanation: `new TArray($generic_params, ...)` is guarded by `if (count($generic_params) !== 2) throw` (line 655), so $generic_params is exactly list{Union,Union}. pzoom does not apply count()===2 narrowing, so it still sees list{Union,Union,...<Union>}.

issues.message: Argument 1 of Psalm\Type\Atomic\TNonEmptyArray::__construct expects list{Psalm\Type\Union, Psalm\Type\Union}, but list{Psalm\Type\Union, Psalm\Type\Union, ...<Psalm\Type\Union>} provided
issues.explanation: `new TNonEmptyArray($generic_params, ...)` — same count()!==2 guard at line 655 fixes the length at 2; pzoom does not narrow via count(), so the tuple looks longer than the expected list{Union,Union}.

issues.message: Argument 1 of Psalm\Type\Atomic\TIterable::__construct expects list{0?: Psalm\Type\Union, 1?: Psalm\Type\Union}, but list{0: Psalm\Type\Union, 1?: Psalm\Type\Union, ...<Psalm\Type\Union>} provided
issues.explanation: `new TIterable($generic_params, ...)` is guarded by `if (count($generic_params) > 2) throw` (line 738). pzoom does not narrow list length through the count()>2 guard, so the argument looks like it may exceed list{0?:Union,1?:Union}.

issues.message: Possibly undefined array key
issues.explanation: `[$seconds, $nseconds] = hrtime();` — Psalm types hrtime() (no/false arg) as the precise array{0:int,1:int}, so 0/1 exist. pzoom's stub returns coarse `array|int|float|false`, so the destructured offsets look possibly-undefined.

issues.message: Property Psalm\Type\Atomic\TKeyedArray::$fallback_params expects list{Psalm\Type\Union, Psalm\Type\Union}|null, possibly different type non-empty-array<int, Psalm\Type\Union>|null provided
issues.explanation: `$fallback_params` is typed list{Union,Union}|null and assigned a non-empty-array<int,Union>|null. In context the value is a 2-element list; Psalm emits the milder PropertyTypeCoercion here (covered by an inline `@psalm-suppress PropertyTypeCoercion`). pzoom emits the more severe PossiblyInvalidPropertyAssignmentValue instead (not covered by that suppress) because it does not treat the non-empty-array as a list shape. Same list-shape root cause as the TypeParser InvalidArgument FPs.

Psalm emits PropertyTypeCoercion (inline-suppressed); pzoom emits PossiblyInvalidPropertyAssignmentValue because it doesn't treat the non-empty-array as a list shape.

issues.message: Redundant cast to string given docblock-provided type
issues.explanation: `(string) key($reference_graph[$references_to_fix[0]])` — $reference_graph is array<string,array<string,true>>; the inner array is possibly-empty, so key() returns string|null and the (string) cast is meaningful. pzoom models key()'s return as string (dropping the null returned for an empty array), making the cast look redundant.

issues.message: Class PHPUnit\Framework\MockObject\Builder\InvocationMocker does not exist
issues.explanation: PHPUnit\Framework\MockObject\Builder\InvocationMocker DOES exist (vendor/phpunit/phpunit/src/Framework/MockObject/Builder/InvocationMocker.php) and Psalm resolves it. pzoom fails to scan/resolve this PHPUnit class, so expects()/method() chains lose their type.

issues.message: Cannot call method withConsecutive on mixed type
issues.explanation: Cascade of the InvocationMocker resolution failure: because pzoom treats the mock builder chain as mixed, `withConsecutive` looks like a call on mixed. withConsecutive exists in the installed PHPUnit and Psalm reports nothing here.

issues.message: Docblock-defined class or interface PHPUnit\Framework\MockObject\Builder\InvocationMocker does not exist
issues.explanation: Docblock references PHPUnit\Framework\MockObject\Builder\InvocationMocker, which exists in vendor and is resolved by Psalm. pzoom fails to resolve the class.

issues.message: Docblock-defined class or interface PHPUnit\Framework\MockObject\Builder\InvocationMocker does not exist
issues.explanation: Docblock references PHPUnit\Framework\MockObject\Builder\InvocationMocker, which exists in vendor and is resolved by Psalm. pzoom fails to resolve the class.

issues.message: Suppression of NullPropertyFetch is never used
issues.explanation: False negative (manifesting as a spurious UnusedPsalmSuppress): the `@psalm-suppress NullPropertyFetch` is genuinely required — Psalm emits NullPropertyFetch here and the suppression consumes it (Psalm's findUnusedPsalmSuppress flags only ONE unused suppression codebase-wide, at Cli/Psalm.php:1071, so every other suppression is in use). pzoom does NOT emit NullPropertyFetch at this spot, so it wrongly concludes the suppression is unused. The underlying defect is that pzoom should emit NullPropertyFetch here but does not; closing that gap would both surface NullPropertyFetch and make this UnusedPsalmSuppress disappear.

issues.message: Cannot find any references to property Psalm\Tests\TypeReconciliation\ReconcilerTest::$file_analyzer
issues.explanation: ReconcilerTest::$file_analyzer IS read at ReconcilerTest.php:55 `$this->file_analyzer->context = new Context();` and :57 (passed as an argument). pzoom misses the read.
